title: hebeclinium recreense
intro: Hebeclinium recreense, commonly known as the "Gordolobo", is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting expectoration. The therapeutic actions of Hebeclinium recreense include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antitussive properties, which contribute to its use in treating respiratory infections and other health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and are being studied for their potential in developing new treatments for various diseases.
side_effects: Hebeclinium recreense can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps. Additionally, improper use of the plant may also lead to allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause liver and kidney damage, as well as affect the nervous system, leading to symptoms like headaches and dizziness. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using any plant-based remedy to avoid these potential side effects.
